remember: Students will be able to recall the meaning of microeconomics and its scope.
Students will be able to explain the difference between macroeconomics and microeconomics.
Students will be able to list the assumptions of microeconomics.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of opportunity cost and its significance in decision-making.
Students will be able to explain the law of diminishing marginal utility and its implications on consumer behavior.
Students will be able to analyze the concept of scarcity and its relationship with choice.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concept of budget constraint to make optimal consumption decisions.
Students will be able to use the concept of indifference curve to analyze consumer behavior.
Students will be able to apply the concept of marginal benefit and marginal cost to make optimal production decisions.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the impact of price changes on consumer behavior and demand.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of production costs on firm's output and pricing decisions.
Students will be able to analyze the trade-offs between different factors of production.

ncert_objectives: To understand the concept of scarcity and its relationship with choice.
To explain the assumptions of microeconomics.
To understand the concept of opportunity cost and its significance in decision-making.
To explain the law of diminishing marginal utility and its implications on consumer behavior.
board_exam_objectives: To recall the meaning and scope of microeconomics.
To explain the difference between macroeconomics and microeconomics.
To list the assumptions of microeconomics.
To apply the concepts to real-life scenarios and case studies.
